# U3: `mtt add --json` emits the created task object (not the plain `created t1`),
# so an agent gets the fresh id without parsing prose (`... --json | jq -r .id`).

exec mtt init

# The default type requires a parent, so create the first task with --no-parent.
exec mtt --json add 'first task' --no-parent
stdout '"id": "t1"'
stdout '"status": "tbd"'
! stdout 'created t1'

# Without --json, the plain ack is unchanged.
exec mtt add 'second task' --no-parent
stdout 'created t2'
